title: Apicra aspera
taxonomy: Apicra aspera is a plant species that belongs to the family Aizoaceae. It is a succulent plant native to South Africa. The classification of Apicra aspera is further divided into the genus Apicra and the species A. aspera. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics, such as its leaf shape and flower color. The Aizoaceae family is a large and diverse group of succulent plants found in various parts of the world.

intro: Apicra aspera, also known as prickly poppy, is a plant species within the Solanaceae family that is native to the Americas. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Apicra aspera include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
